scope: FQ-10: six depth-banded ore families as data plus data-defined generation; no station chain, recipes, save-schema change, or gameplay consumer (those are FQ-11).
summary: The underground gained real mining goals. Six ore families live in data/blocks.json - coal, copper_ore, tin_ore at the tier-1 pick, and iron_ore, silver_ore, crystal behind the existing tier-2 forged-pick gate - each dropping itself, pick-preferred, solid and light-blocking. Generation is entirely data: a new top-level ore_table in world_settings.json gives every family a depth band, a noise frequency, a threshold, and a unique seed_offset. WorldGen builds one FastNoiseLite per family (seed = world_seed + seed_offset) and, for any cell that would otherwise be stone, assigns the first family whose depth band contains it and whose channel clears its threshold. Crucially the generic 'ore' vein decision runs first and is byte-identical, so families only claim leftover stone and every prior ore assertion still holds. ore_abundance lowers all thresholds (richer worlds expose more) and 0 disables all ore. A calibration bug was caught by the smoke and fixed in-run: the first thresholds (0.78-0.87) sat above FastNoiseLite's practical output ceiling (~0.75), so coal generated only 11 cells and the deeper ores zero; recalibrating to 0.58-0.72 produced healthy counts (coal 427, copper 219, tin 220, iron 117, silver+crystal 58 in the large rich test world). Distinct fallback BLOCK_COLORS and items.json swatches make the ores read until block art lands via the image-first pipeline. No save-schema change - ores are terrain cells regenerated from seed; mined cells persist as normal air deltas.
acceptance: Six ore families generate at meaningful counts across shallow/mid/deep bands in a large rich world (fq10_ore_families_generate)
The generic starter 'ore' vein survives alongside the families (fq10_generic_ore_preserved)
Ore-family layout is deterministic across two same-seed setups and never saved (fq10_ore_families_deterministic)
Tier gate holds: iron needs the tier-2 pick, coal is minable at tier-1 (fq10_ore_tier_gate)
ore_abundance 0 clears every ore - families and generic vein alike (fq10_ore_abundance_zero_clears_all)
Suite 262/262; validator (ore blocks + ore_table contract) and capsule doctor green
validation: python scripts/validate_repo.py -> PASS (exit 0), incl. 'ore family blocks' and 'ore table generation contract'
COHERONIA_SMOKE=1 waited headless run -> 262/262 PASS, 5 new fq10_* checks green
git diff --check -> 0 whitespace errors (LF->CRLF notices only)
decisions: Generic 'ore' kept as the tier-2 starter vein and left byte-identical; families only fill stone, preserving all prior ore checks and old-world compatibility
Ore generation table is a top-level world_settings authority (not per-preset) so presets scale it via ore_abundance without overriding it
Deeper ores (iron/silver/crystal) held at the reachable tier-2 gate; shallow starters (coal/copper/tin) at tier-1 - no ore needs a pick above the forged tier
Ores drop themselves with no consumer yet - usefulness deferred to FQ-11, matching 'avoid making every ore immediately useful'
Thresholds calibrated empirically to FastNoiseLite's real range after the smoke caught the first over-high pass
Self-review only (no agent spawned) for a bounded data+generation change fully covered by validator + smoke gates
risks: Pre-FQ-10 worlds regenerate with ore families where plain stone sat (deterministic; deltas overlay cleanly); cosmetic/economy only
No bespoke windowed dig-down screenshot captured this run; ores render via the validated fallback path with distinct colors, but the underground palette was not visually reviewed
Ore density feel at default ore_abundance is untested by human play; all knobs live in ore_table
New ores have no recipe consumer until FQ-11
next_action: FQ-11: workbench/furnace/anvil station chain (first ore consumer). Ore block art via docs/ASSET_ROADMAP.md.
